mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dragonfly is a cloud-native solution that provides efficient, stable, and secure data distribution and acceleration powered by P2P technology. It's particularly useful for improving the delivery of large files, container images, and other artifacts in cloud-native architectures.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflyoss.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dragonfly in a new namespace called 'dragonfly'.
```bash
helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.6.14
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Dragonfly pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dragonfly
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Dragonfly deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment dragonfly --namespace dragonfly -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"dragonfly","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Dragonfly service to access it.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Dragonfly release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any custom resource definitions (CRDs) related to Dragonfly.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep dragonfly | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Dragonfly namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Dragonfly to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace dragonfly
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of Dragonfly before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace dragonfly -o yaml > dragonfly-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Dragonfly Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Dragonfly release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.6.14
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the upgraded Dragonfly pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace dragonfly
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the Dragonfly pods are stuck in Pending state, check for insufficient resources in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace dragonfly
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Dragonfly service, ensure that the service is running and check the port forwarding command.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace dragonfly
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace dragonfly
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage, consider adjusting the resource limits set for the Dragonfly deployment.
```bash
kubectl get deployment dragonfly --namespace dragonfly -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dragonfly will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the service through the specified port. Resource limits should be set appropriately to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflyoss.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed.
